message:
Fix critical edge cases in core library search and export

This commit addresses three important issues found during deep code review:

1. BM25 Search - Prevent NaN scores with zero-length documents
   File: packages/core/src/search/bm25.ts (line 74)

   Problem: When all indexed prompts have empty content, avgDocLength
   becomes 0, causing division by zero in the BM25 length normalization
   formula (doc.length / avgDocLength), resulting in NaN scores.

   Fix: Use Math.max() to ensure avgDocLength is always at least 1:
   avgDocLength: Math.max(totalLength / prompts.length || 1, 1)

2. YAML Export - Add missing control character escaping
   File: packages/core/src/export/yaml.ts (lines 14-15, 51-52, 63-64)

   Problem: Tab (\t) and carriage return (\r) characters were not
   escaped in YAML output, potentially creating invalid YAML files
   that would fail to parse.

   Fix: Add .replace(/\r/g, "\\r") and .replace(/\t/g, "\\t") to
   escapeYamlArrayItem(), and add tab/CR detection to escapeYamlValue().
   Also improved whitespace detection with regex: /^\s/ and /\s$/

3. Tokenizer - Add Unicode normalization for consistent matching
   File: packages/core/src/search/tokenize.ts (lines 24, 37)

   Problem: Identical-looking text with different Unicode representations
   (e.g., "café" vs "cafe\u0301") would fail to match during search
   because the byte representations differ.

   Fix: Add .normalize("NFC") to both tokenize() and tokenizeRaw()
   functions to convert all text to composed Unicode form before
   tokenization, ensuring consistent matching.

All changes are backwards compatible and improve robustness without
changing the expected behavior for normal inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -4,13 +4,15 @@
 
 /**
  * Escape a string for safe use in a YAML double-quoted array element.
- * Handles backslashes, double quotes, and newlines.
+ * Handles backslashes, double quotes, newlines, tabs, and carriage returns.
  */
 export function escapeYamlArrayItem(value: string): string {
   return value
     .replace(/\\/g, "\\\\")
     .replace(/"/g, '\\"')
-    .replace(/\n/g, "\\n");
+    .replace(/\n/g, "\\n")
+    .replace(/\r/g, "\\r")
+    .replace(/\t/g, "\\t");
 }
 
 // YAML 1.1 boolean values that need quoting
@@ -41,11 +43,13 @@ export function escapeYamlValue(value: string): string {
     return '""';
   }
 
-  // Check if value needs quoting (contains special chars, newlines, or starts/contains special chars)
+  // Check if value needs quoting (contains special chars, control chars, or reserved patterns)
   if (
     value.includes(":") ||
     value.includes("#") ||
     value.includes("\n") ||
+    value.includes("\r") ||   // Carriage return
+    value.includes("\t") ||   // Tab character
     value.includes('"') ||
     value.includes("'") ||
     value.includes("[") ||
@@ -56,8 +60,8 @@ export function escapeYamlValue(value: string): string {
     value.includes("|") ||
     value.includes("\\") ||
     value.includes(",") ||    // Flow context separator
-    value.startsWith(" ") ||
-    value.endsWith(" ") ||
+    /^\s/.test(value) ||      // Leading whitespace (space, tab, etc.)
+    /\s$/.test(value) ||      // Trailing whitespace
     value.startsWith("@") ||
     value.startsWith("!") ||
     value.startsWith("&") ||

@@ -15,21 +15,26 @@ const STOPWORDS = new Set([
 ]);
 
 /**
- * Tokenize text into lowercase words, removing stopwords and punctuation
+ * Tokenize text into lowercase words, removing stopwords and punctuation.
+ * Uses NFC normalization to ensure consistent matching for Unicode characters
+ * (e.g., "café" composed vs "cafe\u0301" decomposed are treated identically).
  */
 export function tokenize(text: string): string[] {
   return text
+    .normalize("NFC") // Normalize Unicode to composed form for consistent matching
     .toLowerCase()
     .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, " ") // Remove punctuation except hyphens
     .split(/\s+/)
     .filter((word) => word.length > 1 && !STOPWORDS.has(word));
 }
 
 /**
- * Tokenize without removing stopwords (for exact matching)
+ * Tokenize without removing stopwords (for exact matching).
+ * Uses NFC normalization for consistent Unicode handling.
  */
 export function tokenizeRaw(text: string): string[] {
   return text
+    .normalize("NFC")
     .toLowerCase()
     .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, " ")
     .split(/\s+/)
